name: [rail-archiver] Feed went dark
message:
Feed `{{feed.name}}` (normal cadence) has not polled in the last 15+ minutes. Slow feeds (interval_class:slow, e.g. Bay Area 511) are covered separately by `Slow feed went dark`.

Most common causes:
- Upstream URL changed (check agency dev portal)
- API key expired (check `.env`)
- DNS / network issue

Investigate:
```
docker compose logs app --since 30m | grep {{feed.name}}
```

@<your-notification-target>

name: [rail-archiver] Batch step errored in last 24h
query: sum(last_1d):sum:rollup.day.duration.count{env:dev, service:rail-archiver, status:error}.as_count() + sum:ship.cold.duration.count{env:dev, service:rail-archiver, status:error}.as_count() + sum:ship.hot.duration.count{env:dev, service:rail-archiver, status:error}.as_count() > 0
message:
One or more batch operations (rollup or ship) failed in the last 24h.

Total errors across rollup.day + ship.cold + ship.hot: {{value}}

Investigate:
```
docker compose logs batch --since 24h | grep -i 'error\|traceback'
```

This is a once-a-day batch — a single failure can lose a day's worth of S3 uploads if not retried.

@<your-notification-target>

name: [rail-archiver] Schema drift detected
message:
Decoder schema drift detected on feed `{{feed.name}}` — the incoming payload is missing required keys, so its records are being dropped from the rollup.

Most common causes:
- Upstream agency renamed or removed a JSON key
- The decoder's `required_input_keys` is out of date relative to the live API

Investigate:
```
grep drift_missing_required archive/{{feed.name}}/metadata/year=*/month=*/day=*/data.jsonl | tail
```

@<your-notification-target>

name: [rail-archiver] Poller heartbeat absent
message:
The poller has emitted no `poller.heartbeat` in the last 5 minutes — the process is down or hung.

Unlike `Feed went dark` (which is per-feed), this is the single unambiguous "the archiver is not running" alert. An 18h outage once went unnoticed because nothing watched for this.

Most common causes:
- Poller container crashed and is crash-looping (check `restart` count)
- Poller hung mid-request (autoheal should have restarted it — check why it didn't)
- datadog-agent down, so the heartbeat never reached Datadog

Investigate:
```
docker compose ps
docker compose logs app --since 15m
```

@<your-notification-target>

name: [rail-archiver] Poll error rate elevated
query: avg(last_30m):( sum:feed.poll.duration.count{env:dev, service:rail-archiver, status:error}.as_count() / sum:feed.poll.duration.count{env:dev, service:rail-archiver}.as_count() ) * 100 > 5
message:
Poll error rate is {{value}}% across all feeds over the last 30 minutes.

If only one feed is failing, you'll also see a `Feed went dark` alert. If this fires alone, suspect a systemic issue:
- Outbound network from the container
- DNS resolver
- Auth refresh logic
- Or a transient where many agencies' feeds are down at once (not unusual on weekends)

Drill in:
- Dashboard "Rail Archiver — Pipeline Health" → row 2 → "Poll errors by feed and error_type"

@<your-notification-target>

name: [rail-archiver] Feed quarantined (dead feed)
message:
Feed `{{feed.name}}` has been QUARANTINED after repeated consecutive failures — the poller gave up on its normal cadence and dropped it to a long fallback interval (~1h), so it is effectively producing no fresh data until it recovers.

Unlike a transient blip, quarantine means the feed failed K times in a row. Most common causes:
- Upstream URL changed or the endpoint was retired (check the agency dev portal)
- API key expired or revoked (check `.env`)
- Agency is down for an extended window

Investigate:
```
docker compose logs app --since 2h | grep -i 'quarantined\|{{feed.name}}'
tail archive/{{feed.name}}/metadata/year=*/month=*/day=*/data.jsonl
```

@<your-notification-target>

name: [rail-archiver] Archiver internal error
message:
An UNEXPECTED exception fired inside `archive_one` for feed `{{feed.name}}` ({{value}} in 30m).

This is NOT a transport/HTTP failure — those are caught and recorded as TransportErrorResponse and surface via `Feed went dark` / `Poll error rate`. A `poll.error` means something archiver-side broke: a parsing/writing bug, or a local-disk / write-path failure (disk full, permissions). The self-protecting `_on_done` keeps the loop alive, but this poll's data may have been lost.

Investigate:
```
docker compose logs app --since 30m | grep -A20 'unexpected error polling'
df -h   # rule out a full disk on the write path
```

@<your-notification-target>

name: [rail-archiver] Slow feed went dark
message:
Slow-cadence feed `{{feed.name}}` has not polled in the last 90 minutes. These feeds (interval_class:slow) poll every ~17-30 min by design — often because they share a rate-limited key (e.g. Bay Area 511 at 60 req/hr) — so they get a longer dark window than normal feeds. 90 min with zero polls means it is genuinely stuck, not just slow.

Most common causes:
- Upstream URL changed or endpoint retired
- Shared API key expired/revoked (one bad key darkens the whole agency)
- The agency's rate budget is so saturated the feed never wins a token (check `poll.rate_limited` on the dashboard)

Investigate:
```
docker compose logs app --since 2h | grep {{feed.name}}
```

@<your-notification-target>

name: [rail-archiver] Ingest saturated (polls being shed)
message:
The poll loop shed {{value}} polls to the concurrency gate (`poll.skipped`) in the last 15 min — every worker slot was busy, so feeds were skipped rather than queued.

With the startup herd fixed (seed spread), steady-state poll.skipped should be ~0. Sustained shedding means the box can't keep up: polls are taking longer than expected, or there are simply too many feeds for the worker pool.

NOTE: this threshold (100/15m) is a first guess with no baseline yet — tune it against the dashboard once you've watched normal load. This is a CAPACITY signal, not an outage: data isn't lost, but coverage is thinning.

Levers:
- Raise `--workers` (more concurrent polls)
- Lengthen poll intervals on low-value feeds
- Shard feeds across processes (roadmap step 4)

@<your-notification-target>
